title: Console server saves and restores UI state from .kanbus/.cache/console_state.json
description:
Given a console server that has been running with some UI state
(e.g., an issue is focused, a view mode is set)

When the server is stopped and restarted

Then the server reads the last-pushed route from .kanbus/.cache/console_state.json at startup
And replays that route to clients that connect via SSE
And kbs console get focus returns the same focused issue as before the restart

Acceptance criteria:
- ConsoleUiState (the cache of the last-pushed route) is persisted to .kanbus/.cache/console_state.json after every mutation
- State is loaded from that file at server startup (file missing = default empty state, no error)
- File format is JSON, human-readable
- On restart, the restored state is replayed to new SSE subscribers as a route push
creator: ryan.porter

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implemented: ConsoleUiState persisted to .kanbus/.cache/console_state.json after every mutation. Loaded at server startup (missing file = empty state, no error). get_console_state_path() added to daemon_paths.rs following existing cache path pattern.
